g.text: Unique identifier consisting of the alpha-3 country code and ID record of the question
List of questions used in the Questionnaire, structured around 12 themes related to choice of law
Predetermined answer options to the Questions (see column Answering Options in the \Questions\ tab).
Further elaboration on the predetermined Answers
Internal field to highlight jurisdictionally unique Answers
Internal field to indicate that the Answer might not be accurate
Legal provisions connected to the answer
Legal provisions of secondary importance to the Answer (mentioned in case law records)
The main instrument that encompasses the legal provisions or the legal source of the Answer
Case law providing information to the corresponding Questions
Automated field connected to the Questions Tab
Automated field connected to the Questions Tab
Automated field connected to the Jurisdictions Tab
Automated field connected to the Jurisdictions Tab
Automated field connected to the Jurisdictions Tab
Automated field connected to the Interfaces
Field connected to the display of answers in the Interface
Unique identifier consisting of a unique number and the abbreviation of the Theme
Framework for data collection defined by the research specialists
Alternative wording to be displayed in the website (AB)
A jurisdictional record can be classified as \State\, \Financial\ (district), \Supraregional\, \Regional\ or \Federal\ according to the legal nature of its rules.
More comprehensive regional classification according to the purposes of this research project
0 = no answer; 1 = answer has been filled out
Jurisdictions that will never be filled out
Unique identifier consisting of the first three characters from the title and a unique number
Legal instruments in English starting with the adjective (Demonym) related to the jurisdiction
Title of the legal instrument in the respective language of the jurisdiction
Checkbox to indicate whether the HCCH Principles are incorporated in the legal instrument
The date that the legal instrument was published
The date that the legal instrument became effective in that jurisdiction
Website where the legal instrument is available
Unique identifier consisting of the corresponding legislation and article
Number or part of the specific legal provision
Automated link to answers for secondary legal provisions
Unique identifier consisting of the Alpha-3 country code and unique number for the case law decision

This field contains information on the Issuing Court, the Claimant and Respondent's names, the Number ID of the case, and the date when the decision was issued. The title of the case varies according to the jurisdiction.
Questions answered by this case for the corresponding jurisdiction
Website where the case law decision is available
Official abstract of the decision, otherwise AI-generated

The relevant legal provisions that are related to the choice of law from the choice of law issue(s)/agreement/clause/interpretation(s). This field might also include important precedents or other decisions that were used as a reference in the judgment.
Questions arising from the choice of law issue(s)/agreement/clause/interpretation(s)
The opinion of the court in regard to the statements made in the \Choice of law issue\ column.
English translation of the \Quote\ column (relevant excerpt of the decision)
Full text of the relevant rules involved. Also found in the \Answers\ tab with further elaboration of the legal provision.
Cases related to topics that are currently outside the scope of the Dataverse
Automated field pertaining to the Questions tab
Questions corresponding to the Answers connected to this case (manually selected)
Excerpt of the decision where the court justifies its position (judgment). The quote is always in the original language of the case/in the words of the court.

A short summary of the facts of the case (who are the parties, what happened, what is the dispute about, the different stages of court proceedings, etc.). This field prioritizes information on choice of law.
Arbitral award preceding this court decision (contested before this Court)
Official name of the arbitral institution
Common abbreviation for the arbitral institution
Jurisdiction in which the arbitral institution has its headquarters
Set of rules that governs the proceedings for institutional arbitrations
Relevant arbitral decisions conducted by the institutional
Title of the instrument from a given arbitral institution
Website where the rules are available
Official case reference designated by the arbitral institution
Responsible arbitral institution for the case
Year that the arbitral award was rendered
This field refers to the procedural stage of the arbitration when the decision was rendered
A short summary of the facts of the case (who are the parties, what happened, what is the dispute about, etc.). This field prioritizes information on choice of law.
If the award is contested, it will be referred to the courts of this jurisdiction
Responsible person to enter the final text
How the first draft of the Definition came about
0 = no answer; 1 = answer has been filled out
